articles.title: Jeu concours : tentez de remporter vos places pour le VivaTech Festival
articles.description: VivaTech fête ses 10 ans avec une journée de festival ouverte au grand public le 20 juin à Paris. BDM met 40 places en jeu sur Instagram !

articles.title: Calendrier scolaire 2026-2027 avec les dates des vacances scolaires des zones A, B et C
articles.description: Consultez le calendrier scolaire 2026-2027 avec l'ensemble des dates importantes de l'année : rentrée, vacances et jours fériés pour les zones A, B et C.

articles.title: IA, link in bio, SEO : 5 nouveaux outils à découvrir sur Product Hunt en mai 2026
articles.description: Un générateur de contenus optimisés pour le SEO, un agent IA qui passe des appels à votre place... Voici cinq outils qui ont retenu l'attention des utilisateurs de Product Hunt en mai 2026.

articles.title: Emploi dans le numérique : « Nous sommes loin de la job apocalypse qu’on nous promet »
articles.description: Alors que les intentions d'embauche reculent dans l'informatique, Véronique Torner, présidente de Numeum, nous partage sa lecture du marché de l'emploi numérique en France en 2026.

articles.title: Benchmark raises its first-ever growth fund as part of $2B capital raise
articles.description: The legendary abandons its more than 20 year tradition of keeping its funds to about $425 million.

articles.title: Quick commerce FirstClub doubles valuation to $255M in nine months
articles.description: The Bengaluru startup has crossed 1 million orders and reached a $50 million annualized GMV run rate within a year of launch.

articles.title: Lovable signs multiyear deal with Google Cloud to up usage 5x, source says
articles.description: Lovable and Google signed an expanded multiyear deal that involves a 5x expansion of Lovable's footprint on Google Cloud, and expanded access to Anthropic Claude.

articles.title: Defense tech is flooded with money, but who’s built to last?
articles.description: Defense tech is red hot right now. Anduril and Mach Industries just doubled and quadrupled their valuations, respectively, and the U.S. government is proposing a 40% increase in defense budget. A wave of new startups is chasing those government contracts, but according to Ross Fubini, the venture…

articles.title: Uber to put 500 data-collection vehicles on the road this year
articles.description: The modified Ioniq 5 will be loaded with sensors to capture data for Uber's new AV Labs division.

articles.title: Alphabet’s record-breaking $85B raise for Google’s AI business is a helluva good signal
articles.description: If Alphabet's record-breaking $85 billion stock sale signals investor appetite for AI-related offerings, we can see that investors are ready to chow.

articles.title: Meta mercifully spun out VR fitness game Supernatural instead of just killing it
articles.description: Meta appears to have listened to the Supernatural users who protested the app's sad fate after sweeping layoffs.

articles.title: Substack’s new ‘Reply Rules’ feature lets creators control how people respond
articles.description: Substack's new Reply Rules feature is currently available for all English-language publications and is designed to give creators greater control over how their audiences respond.

articles.title: Google’s Dreambeans, its weirdest-named AI tool to date, will turn your life into a cartoon
articles.description: Dreambeans is a curated list of AI-illustrated "stories" culled from the personal data in your Google account.

articles.title: Ultrahuman says hackers accessed customers’ wellness data via internal tool
articles.description: The breach at wearable ring maker Ultrahuman stemmed from credentials stolen from a malware-infected employee laptop.

articles.title: Carvana ties up with Bezos-backed Slate Auto as it plans new car sales
articles.description: Carvana was granted a warrant to buy shares in Slate last year, according to documents obtained by TechCrunch. Guggenheim Partners CEO Mark Walter is heavily invested in both companies.

articles.title: Instagram is alerting users who were targeted by hackers during AI chatbot attacks
articles.description: Hackers appeared to take over victims’ accounts even after Meta said it fixed its AI-powered support chatbot, which granted hackers access to victims’ accounts.

articles.title: Amazon will show AI product images when you search for some reason
articles.description: Amazon will use visual search and AI to show AI-generated product images that match your search queries. The retailer says it will help guide users to products.

articles.title: Still facing copyright lawsuits, AI music generator Suno raises another $400M
articles.description: The prominent AI music-generation startup is now valued at over $5.4 billion -- about seven months ago, it raised at a $2.45 billion valuation.

articles.title: These two founders left Goldman and Meta to build voice AI for markets everyone else overlooked
articles.description: The startup's own stack for Africa and Middle East is now handling more than 17,000 calls per day.

articles.title: GitLab cuts 14% of staff as it scales its platform to serve AI workloads
articles.description: The company is reducing its workforce as it exits 22 countries, reduces management layers, and invests in its infrastructure to scale its platform.

articles.title: Publishers will be able to opt out of AI Search, thanks to new regulation
articles.description: U.K. regulators are requiring Google offer a tool allowing website publishers to opt-out of generative AI search features. The option will be tested in the U.K. then rolled out globally.

articles.title: TikTok launches TikTok Pro Events, an app for cultural moments like the FIFA World Cup
articles.description: The app allows users to engage with other fans, explore trending videos, and access curated creator feeds.

articles.title: The worst hacks and breaches of 2026 (so far)
articles.description: From a massive DOGE data breach and the hacking of critical energy and water systems to the hack of an FBI surveillance system, here are the most damaging security incidents and data breaches of 2026.

articles.title: Meet Wander, a StumbleUpon-inspired tool for discovering the ‘small web’
articles.description: This open source community project lets you create a StumbleUpon-like experience for recommending your favorite sites.

articles.title: Codex s’ouvre aux analystes, marketeurs et financiers avec 6 nouveaux plugins
articles.description: OpenAI déploie six plugins spécifiques à chaque métier pour Codex, accompagnés de deux nouveautés : Sites et annotations. Le but est de rendre l'agent utile aux équipes non techniques, qui représentent déjà 20 % de ses utilisateurs.

articles.title: Microsoft dévoile Scout, un agent IA autonome bâti sur OpenClaw pour les entreprises
articles.description: Disponible en version preview via le programme Frontier, Scout opère en continu en arrière-plan et peut notamment planifier des réunions ou générer des documents préparatoires.

articles.title: Microsoft lance 7 modèles d’IA et se détache un peu plus d’OpenAI
articles.description: À l'occasion de Microsoft Build, la firme a dévoilé une famille de sept modèles développés en interne. MAI-Thinking-1, le premier modèle de raisonnement maison, constitue la pièce maîtresse de cette annonce.

articles.title: La vaste blague du macronisme crypto-spatial
articles.description: Deux semaines en orbite pour deux astronautes français, Thomas Pesquet et Arnaud Prost. Voilà ce qu’a négocié le Mozart de la finance : un plan annoncé en grande pompe au sommet Choose France, ce 1er juin, sur fond de souveraineté industrielle et La vaste blague du macronisme crypto-spatial first…

articles.title: Le monde merveilleux des ADR !
articles.description: Les "Architecture Decision Record" font partie de la documentation de votre projet, et peuvent vous sauver la vie ! Notes de l'épisode : Le bus factor : https://code-garage.com/podcast/classic/episode-53

articles.title: PDF Spaces : comment un freelance peut structurer son activité marketing et commerciale
articles.description: Quand on travaille seul(e), chaque minute compte. Préparation de pitch client, centralisation de la veille, partage d'un dossier structuré avec un prospect... Voici comment PDF Spaces peut devenir le hub central d'un ou une auto-entrepreneur.

articles.title: Tout sur Symfony 8.1
articles.description: Symfony 8.1 vient de sortir, et fidèle à sa promesse, le framework garantit une rétrocompatibilité totale, permettant une mise à jour transparente. Cette release apporte également des changements de paradigme majeurs : elle pousse encore plus loin l’utilisation des Attributs PHP, simplifie…

articles.title: Que faire après un métier de technicien systèmes et réseaux ?
articles.description: Cybersécurité, cloud computing, DevOps, gestion de projet IT... Après plusieurs années en tant que technicien systèmes et réseaux, les pistes d'évolution sont nombreuses. Tour d'horizon des opportunités à saisir ou à envisager.

articles.title: La vision de Microsoft sur le rôle des équipes marketing et l’IA dans la création publicitaire
articles.description: Product Liaison chez Microsoft Advertising, Navah Hopkins détaille sa vision du rôle des équipes face aux systèmes d'IA dans la chaîne de création publicitaire. Elle interviendra à Intelligence Marketing Day, un événement en partenariat avec BDM.

articles.title: #359.src - Devenir tech lead: Ne rien lâcher avec Houleymatou Baldé
articles.description: "J'ai toujours su, quand je veux quelque chose, aller le chercher, mais surtout argumenter" Le D.E.V. de la semaine est Houleymatou Baldé, ex-Tech Lead et fondatrice de Yeeso. Venue en France sans prérequis informatiques, Houleymatou raconte comment elle est devenue tech lead après un parcours semé…

articles.title: News Juin 2026, Bun passe à Rust, npm verrouille les scripts et SEO pour l'IA
articles.description: Nous évoquons le portage de Bun de Zig vers Rust par des agents IA, npm qui bloque par défaut les scripts postinstall non approuvés, Composer 2.10 qui filtre nativement les paquets malveillants, Windsurf absorbé par Devin Desktop, le guide SEO de Google optimisé pour les IA, MCP Chrome DevTools,…

articles.title: Squishmallows, dentures, and an ‘I Heart Hot Dads’ bag: Uber has found thousands of items left in robotaxis
articles.description: Even in a future of robot taxis, someone still has to return the things passengers leave behind.

articles.title: Cyera eyes $12B valuation at 80x ARR multiple despite operating losses

articles.title: Cyberdecks are having a moment, rejecting big tech surveillance with style and substance
articles.description: Over the last few months, these DIY hardware communities have exploded in popularity as people on social media show off their solar-powered game emulators, pocket-sized ereaders, and clamshell purse computers.

articles.title: Uber caps employee AI spending after blowing through budget in 4 months
articles.description: Uber's cutback has occurred after the company had reportedly encouraged staff to use AI as much as possible.

articles.title: New Microsoft tool lets devs spin up AI behavior tests using text descriptions

articles.title: A startup, Everand, is now bundling e-books, audiobooks, and book clubs in challenge to Amazon

articles.title: Martin Scorsese becomes the latest — and most unlikely — Hollywood voice for AI

articles.title: Microsoft launches Scout, an OpenClaw-inspired personal assistant

articles.title: Ex-Anduril engineer raises $42M to build the Amazon of composite parts
articles.description: Layup Parts co-founder Zack Eakin has drawn on a motorsports background, and his experience working for Palmer Luckey and Elon Musk, to tackle making faster, cheaper, and better composites.

articles.title: Google rolls out fake call detection to protect against AI deepfake impersonation scams
articles.description: As people increasingly refuse to answer calls from unknown numbers, scammers are shifting their tactics by spoofing trusted phone numbers and using AI deepfake technology to sound like authority figures, family members, or employers.

articles.title: Microsoft offers devs a better way to control AI agent behavior
articles.description: The specification lets developer, compliance, and security teams define their own policies for agents to follow in portable policy files.

articles.title: Amazon faces class action lawsuit over Ring facial-recognition feature
articles.description: The class action lawsuit, filed in Seattle by Virginia resident Charles Sigwalt, claims that Ring's Familiar Faces feature stores images of passersby without consent.

articles.title: Focused Energy raises whopping $240M Series A for laser-powered fusion tech
articles.description: Another fusion startup has raised another a massive round to make this type of power a reality.

articles.title: Trump signs narrower executive order on AI oversight after industry objections
articles.description: After industry objections, President Trump signed a revised AI executive order requiring only voluntary prerelease government reviews of advanced models.

articles.title: OpenAI launches new Codex tools for white-collar work
articles.description: OpenAI released a set of six plug-ins aimed at specific jobs: data analytics, creative production, sales, product design, equity investing, and investment banking. Available from within the Codex app, each of the new tools bundles integrations, instructions, and context to allow Codex to…

articles.title: Board, the new game startup from Mirror founder Brynn Putnam, raises $20M, has already sold thousands
articles.description: Board, the startup building what it calls "together tech" designed to bring people into the same room, has closed a Series A led by Union Square Ventures.

articles.title: Password manager Dashlane says hackers stole some customers’ password vaults
articles.description: The password manager giant said hackers were able to "brute-force" its two-factor system, allowing them to access customer accounts and download their password vaults.

articles.title: Why VivaTech 2026 is the place to see Europe’s AI strategy take shape
articles.description: TechCrunch is partnering with VivaTech 2026 to spotlight some of the most important conversations shaping the future of artificial intelligence. Join us in Paris June 17-20!

articles.title: Meta tests ‘Series’ for episodic Reels on Instagram and Facebook
articles.description: Meta told TechCrunch that it's considering ways to monetize the new feature, but didn't share specifics on what that could look like.

articles.title: X caters to creators with new ‘React with Video’ feature
articles.description: X will now let you 'react with video' to posts.

articles.title: Les 100 principaux cas d’usage de l’IA en 2026
articles.description: Entre un usage professionnel aux gains modestes et un usage personnel toujours plus intime, l'IA décline ses usages dans toutes les sphères du quotidien.

articles.title: We Are Digital Producers : qu’est-ce qu’un collectif digital efficace et créatif ?
articles.description: À quoi correspond un collectif de digital producers, à quels besoins du marché répond-il, pour quels types de missions ? Cet article fait suite à un entretien avec l’équipe de We Are Digital Producers et fait le point sur ce qu’un collectif d’experts digitaux peut apporter et dans quel contexte.…

articles.title: ChatGPT : comment régler le niveau d’effort pour préserver ses limites d’usage
articles.description: OpenAI ajoute à ChatGPT un geste "caché" pour choisir le niveau de réflexion de chaque message, entre Instant, Thinking et Extended.

articles.title: GitHub Copilot bascule sur une facturation à l’usage : ce qui change pour les développeurs
articles.description: GitHub adopte un modèle de facturation basé sur la consommation réelle pour tous ses plans Copilot. Les crédits remplacent les requêtes premium, avec de nouveaux outils de contrôle des dépenses à la clé.

articles.title: Le luxe à l’heure de l’IA : face à la standardisation algorithmique, le pari de l’excellence humaine
articles.description: Depuis 2023, le luxe ne souffre pas d'une crise de marché, mais d'une crise de désirabilité. À l’occasion de la sortie de Luxe Renaissance, Éric Briones (Journal du Luxe) et Vincent Montet (EFAP) décryptent ce que le secteur doit préserver pour renaître.

articles.title: Newsletter Mai 2026
articles.description: Je déteste le mois de mai. Pas à cause de la météo pourrie (sauf la dernière semaine), mais à cause de ces ponts et jours fériés qui donne l’impression que toute la France est en vacances et qu’il est impossible de faire avancer les projets. Que faire, pendant ce temps là ? Écrire et faire […]…

articles.title: Tech For Industry : l’industrie 4.0 s’invite à Paris les 23 et 24 juin
articles.description: Les 23 et 24 juin prochains, Paris Expo – Porte de Versailles accueillera dans son hall 5.2 un nouveau rendez-vous professionnel entièrement dédié aux technologies de l’industrie 4.0 : le Tech For Industry Show. Un événement que Robot-Magazine.fr suit en tant que partenaire officiel, et qui…

articles.title: Freelances tech : comment leurs compétences se transforment sous la pression de l’IA
articles.description: Malt publie son rapport Tech Trends 2026, fondé sur l'analyse de 2,5 millions de recherches sur sa plateforme. JavaScript recule, tandis que les agents IA et l'automatisation prennent le dessus.

articles.title: How to make the Startup Battlefield Top 20 — and what every company gets regardless
articles.description: Every founder who applies to Startup Battlefield wants the same thing: the Disrupt Main Stage. Here’s how to get there and why the opportunity starts well before the main stage.

articles.title: Alphabet plans to raise $80B to pay for AI buildout
articles.description: "The company is experiencing strong demand for its AI solutions and services from enterprises and consumers, at levels that are exceeding the company’s available supply," Alphabet said in its statement.

articles.title: Defense tech darling Mach Industries hits $1.8B valuation, a 4x jump in a year
articles.description: In a wild ride for 22-year-old founder and CEO Ethan Thornton, Mach Industries has raised another $300 million. It already has five autonomous vehicles in development and completed a major acquisition.

articles.title: Nvidia chases $200B CPU market with AI agent PCs from Microsoft, Dell, and HP
articles.description: If Nvidia has cracked a way to bring AI agents easily, safely, and usefully to the masses, it could — and should — be big.

articles.title: From the stage to the future: Where are Startup Battlefield’s alumni now?
articles.description: We wanted to show you what happens after the confetti falls. We checked in with some of our recent alumni, many of whom have sat down with us on Build Mode: The Founder Survival Guide, TechCrunch's podcast for founders at every stage.

articles.title: Florida sues OpenAI, Sam Altman, in first-of-its-kind lawsuit over violent incidents
articles.description: The lawsuit partially revolves around a shooting at Florida State University last year, and ChatGPT's alleged role in the incident.

articles.title: Hackers hijacked Instagram accounts by tricking Meta AI support chatbot into granting access
articles.description: Several users on social media reported having their Instagram accounts hacked over the weekend. Meta's own support chatbot was blamed for allowing hackers to hijack accounts.

articles.title: Water access is now a risk factor in SpaceX’s IPO
articles.description: The company says it needs "significant" water resources to cool its data centers, and that access to abundant, affordable water is a challenge.

articles.title: A new app, The Mall, is building a universal feed for online shopping
articles.description: The Mall lets shoppers build a personalized feed of brands, track sales and drops, and discover products across thousands of retailers.

articles.title: Grand Theft Auto V cheat service gets hacked, exposing thousands of gamers
articles.description: Hackers stole usernames, hashed passwords, and other data from a service that allowed players to cheat in Grand Theft Auto V.

articles.title: Zigging when most are zagging, ex-Meta CTO raises $250M climate fund
articles.description: Mike Schroepfer's Gigascale Capital has raised a large fund to back founders building climate-friendly solutions for the world's energy and material shortages.

articles.title: Anthropic files to go public
articles.description: Anthropic, now an AI powerhouse that has landed top-tier enterprise customers, was once considered an underdog in the emerging world of large language models.

articles.title: SpaceX says it may issue ‘significant’ equity in ‘future transactions’
articles.description: The company added a warning to prospective investors that a major dilution could be in the cards after it goes public.

articles.title: This AI weather startup is out-forecasting government agencies
articles.description: WindBorne benefits from its unique combination of model-building and data collection. The company now has about 400 balloons in flight gathering sensor readings at any given time, launched from 15 sites around the globe. The advances in its current model come from improvements in how the data…

articles.title: DuckDuckGo makes its ‘no-AI’ search engine easier to access as its traffic booms
articles.description: Alternative search engine DuckDuckGo launches 'no AI' web extensions for Chrome and Firefox users.

articles.title: Revolut rolls out services to thousands of users in India ahead of broader launch
articles.description: The British fintech has built a waitlist of about 450,000 users in India as it prepares for a broader launch.

articles.description: Trained from scratch and designed for practical deployment, Mellum2 is built for routing, Q&A, sub-agents, and private AI use in software engineering systems. articles.title: Claude : comment régler le niveau d’effort pour économiser des tokens
articles.description: Depuis Claude Opus 4.8, un réglage permet de doser la profondeur de réflexion du modèle. Voici comment l'ajuster pour gagner en rapidité et préserver vos tokens.

articles.title: Strava declares war on scrapers ahead of IPO
articles.description: To stop scraping, the company is increasing security around its website and will now only allow authenticated users to view certain data. Earlier, users were able to see details like public profiles and fitness club listings without logging in. The company is putting all that data behind…

articles.title: Unastella, a South Korean rocket startup that launched from home, raises $24M
articles.description: The Seoul-based rocket startup is developing its own launch vehicles and engines.

articles.title: IA : les meilleurs modèles pour le code et le développement web en juin 2026
articles.description: Toujours dominée par Anthropic, la WebDevArena voit Alibaba revenir en force avec Qwen3.7-max, tandis qu'OpenAI disparaît du top 10.

articles.title: Instagram intègre un prompteur pour les Reels : comment ça marche
articles.description: La fonctionnalité avait été déployée en juin 2025 sur Edits. Elle fait son apparition directement dans l'outil de création d'Instagram.

articles.title: Sarbacane devient Positive User : la plateforme d’emailing change de nom et d’ambition
articles.description: Après vingt-cinq ans d'existence sous la marque Sarbacane, la plateforme française de marketing digital change de nom et annonce une ambition européenne plus marquée. Le nouveau nom, Positive User, est entré en vigueur fin mai 2026.

articles.title: 5 formations pour apprendre à analyser les données
articles.description: Une sélection de formations destinée à celles et ceux souhaitant s'orienter ou se reconvertir dans la data analyse.

articles.title: Facebook a-t-il encore sa place dans une stratégie social media ?
articles.description: Ringard, vieillissant, dépassé... Facebook cumule les clichés. Mais les données et les retours terrain racontent une autre histoire. 5 experts nous partagent leur vision !
